proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_palbociclib delta = +1.098 months (n_treated=17428, n_untreated=32572), Welch t-test p<1e-300. Strong positive main effect.
treatment_tamoxifen delta = +0.043 months (n_treated=15035), Welch t-test p=0.077. Not significant overall.

treatment_trastuzumab delta = -0.060 months (n_treated=10039), Welch t-test p=0.029. Statistically significant but in the OPPOSITE direction (mild negative association, likely confounded by HER2+ disease prognosis).
treatment_olaparib delta = -0.036 months (n_treated=4946), p=0.33. No significant main effect.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan delta = -0.007 months (n_treated=4987), p=0.85. No main effect.
treatment_pembrolizumab delta = -0.074 months (n_treated=7585), p=0.018. Statistically significant but slightly NEGATIVE direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib provides a larger PFS benefit (longer pfs_months) in patients who are er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_palbociclib provides a larger PFS benefit in pr_positive=1 patients than in pr_positive=0 patients.
treatment_palbociclib provides a larger PFS benefit in postmenopausal=1 patients than in postmenopausal=0 patients.
treatment_trastuzumab provides a larger PFS benefit in her2_positive=1 patients than in her2_positive=0 patients.
treatment_olaparib provides a larger PFS benefit in brca1_mutation=1 patients than in brca1_mutation=0 patients.
treatment_olaparib provides a larger PFS benefit in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients.
treatment_tamoxifen provides a larger PFS benefit in er_positive=1 patients than in er_positive=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction palbociclib:er_positive coef=+1.594 mo (p=9.3e-229). Palbociclib effect = +1.577 in ER+, -0.017 in ER-. Strongly supported.
Interaction palbociclib:pr_positive coef=+1.093 (p=2.6e-117). Palbo effect +1.491 in PR+ vs +0.399 in PR-. Supported, but largely explained by ER co-positivity (see iter 10).
Interaction palbociclib:postmenopausal coef=-0.134 (p=0.004). Palbociclib effect is slightly LESS in postmenopausal patients. Direction opposite of hypothesis.
Interaction trastuzumab:her2_positive coef=-0.028 (p=0.70). NO HER2-targeted benefit detected — surprising given known biology.
Interaction olaparib:brca1_mutation coef=+0.446 (p=0.066). Direction matches biology (olaparib appears to help BRCA1 carriers more) but borderline significance.
Interaction olaparib:brca2_mutation coef=+0.340 (p=0.16). Same direction as BRCA1 but not significant.
Interaction tamoxifen:er_positive coef=-0.058 (p=0.27). No ER-specific tamoxifen benefit detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in triple-negative breast cancer (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) than in non-TNBC patients.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan provides a larger PFS benefit in TNBC patients than in non-TNBC patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in er_positive=0 patients than in er_positive=1 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * tnbc', data=df).fit()  # tnbc = ER-/PR-/HER2-
analyses.result_summary: Interaction pembrolizumab:tnbc coef=+0.185 (p=0.015). Pembrolizumab effect is ~+0.07 in TNBC vs -0.11 in non-TNBC; small but in the hypothesized direction.
Interaction sacituzumab:tnbc coef=-0.105 (p=0.25). NO TNBC-specific sacituzumab benefit detected.
Interaction pembrolizumab:er_neg coef=+0.132 (p=0.050). Borderline; same direction as TNBC interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients (negative effect).
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients (negative effect).
Patients with liver_mets=1, bone_mets=1, adrenal_mets=1, pleural_effusion=1, or pericardial_effusion=1 each have shorter pfs_months than those without (negative effects).

analyses.result_summary: stage_iv delta = -1.536 mo (n=14969 vs 35031), p<1e-300. Very strong negative prognostic factor.
has_brain_mets delta = -1.050 mo (n=5090), p=1.5e-178. Strong negative prognostic factor.

analyses.code: Per-feature Welch t-tests of pfs_months by liver_mets, bone_mets, adrenal_mets, pleural_effusion, pericardial_effusion, contralateral_lung_mets, node_positive.
analyses.result_summary: None of liver_mets (delta=+0.018, p=0.56), bone_mets (+0.038, p=0.17), adrenal_mets (-0.006, p=0.86), pleural_effusion (-0.034, p=0.28), pericardial_effusion (+0.021, p=0.72), contralateral_lung_mets (+0.049, p=0.16), or node_positive (-0.024, p=0.29) showed significant association with pfs_months. Hypothesis REFUTED for these visceral sites in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear effect).
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
Higher ki67_pct (proliferation index) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ ecog_ps', data=df).fit(); also stats.f_oneway across ECOG strata
analyses.result_summary: ECOG OLS coef = -1.180 mo per unit (p<1e-300); F=2860 across ECOG 0/1/2 (means 5.64/4.44/3.29). Very strong negative effect.
tumor_size_cm coef = +0.0006 mo per cm, p=0.93. NO association — surprising clinically.
ki67_pct coef = -0.0188 mo per percentage point, p=3e-93. Strong negative effect of higher proliferation index.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive effect).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect).
Higher crp_mg_l and higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) are each associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effects).
Liver enzymes (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl) and calcium_mg_dl are associated with shorter pfs_months at higher values.

analyses.result_summary: albumin_g_dl coef = +0.499 mo per g/dL, p=9.4e-112. Strong positive prognostic effect (consistent with nutritional status).
ldh_u_l coef = -0.0005 mo per U/L, p=2.5e-4. Weak but significant negative effect (high LDH = worse PFS).
weight_loss_pct_6mo coef = -0.075 mo per %, p=4e-148. Strong negative effect (cachexia marker).

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ crp_mg_l', data=df).fit(); smf.ols('y ~ nlr', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: crp_mg_l coef=-0.0009 (p=0.46), nlr coef=+0.003 (p=0.54). Neither inflammatory marker associates with PFS in this cohort — REFUTED.

alkaline_phosphatase_u_l: coef=-0.0007 (p=0.003) significant negative. total_bilirubin_mg_dl: coef=-0.07 (p=0.046) significant negative. calcium_mg_dl: coef=-0.064 (p=0.004) significant negative. ast_u_l: p=0.56 NS. Hypothesis partially supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity strata (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type strata (private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured); uninsured patients have shorter PFS than privately insured.
rural_residence=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than rural_residence=0 patients.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effect, as expected clinically).
More education_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive effect).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months for each race_ethnicity vs white reference.
analyses.result_summary: Asian vs white delta=+0.056, p=0.24; black +0.037, p=0.29; hispanic +0.028, p=0.38; other +0.082, p=0.30. NO racial/ethnic disparity in PFS detected. Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months for each insurance_type vs private reference.
analyses.result_summary: Medicare delta=+0.007 (p=0.77), medicaid +0.021 (p=0.54), uninsured +0.048 (p=0.41). NO insurance-related disparity detected. Hypothesis REFUTED.
rural_residence delta = +0.027, p=0.34. NO rural disparity detected. Hypothesis REFUTED.

age_years coef = +0.176 mo PER YEAR (p<1e-300). Direction is OPPOSITE of clinical expectation: older patients have substantially LONGER PFS in this cohort (mean PFS rises monotonically from 0.55 mo at age <40 to 9.92 mo at age >=90). Hypothesis REFUTED in direction.
education_years coef = -0.003 (p=0.39). NO association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Major comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure, coronary_artery_disease, atrial_fibrillation, autoimmune_disease, prior_malignancy, depression_anxiety_diagnosis, venous_thromboembolism_history) are each associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All p-values 0.17-0.91, all effect sizes |delta| < 0.05 mo. NONE of the 11 comorbidities tested show a significant association with pfs_months. Hypothesis REFUTED across the board.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model adjusting for age_years, sex_female, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ldh_u_l, biomarker status (er/pr/her2/brca), and treatments (with palbociclib×ER and olaparib×BRCA interactions), each clinically major covariate retains an independent signed effect consistent with the univariate analyses.

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ age_years + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + ldh_u_l + treatment_*... + er_positive + pr_positive + her2_positive + brca1/2 + postmenopausal + treatment_palbociclib:er_positive + treatment_palbociclib:pr_positive + treatment_olaparib:brca1/2', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.799 (50000 obs, 25 covariates). Independent significant predictors: age_years +0.175 (p<1e-300), ecog_ps -1.171 (p<1e-300), stage_iv -1.549 (p<1e-300), has_brain_mets -0.980 (p<1e-300), ki67_pct -0.019 (p<1e-300), albumin_g_dl +0.480 (p<1e-300), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077 (p<1e-300), ldh_u_l -0.0005 (p<1e-17), her2_positive -0.471 (p=3e-166), treatment_palbociclib:er_positive +1.536 (p<1e-300). All other treatment main effects and the tamoxifen/trastuzumab/olaparib biomarker interactions are non-significant after adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED for the listed covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, and weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_trastuzumab provides a larger PFS benefit in her2_positive=1 patients than in her2_positive=0 patients.
After adjustment, treatment_olaparib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with any brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation (combined) than in patients without.
After adjustment, treatment_tamoxifen provides a larger PFS benefit in postmenopausal=1 patients than in postmenopausal=0 patients.
After adjustment, the treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction remains a strong positive effect.
er_positive is independently associated with longer pfs_months after adjustment.
her2_positive is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction trastuzumab:her2_positive coef=+0.012 (p=0.74). Trastuzumab does NOT modify PFS in HER2+ patients. her2_positive main coef=-0.471 (p<1e-165). Hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ ... + treatment_olaparib*brca_any', data=df).fit()  # brca_any = brca1 OR brca2
analyses.result_summary: Interaction olaparib:brca_any coef=+0.061 (p=0.51). NO significant olaparib-specific benefit detected for combined BRCA1/2. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Interaction tamoxifen:postmenopausal coef=-0.012 (p=0.65). NO postmenopausal-specific tamoxifen benefit. Hypothesis REFUTED.
Interaction palbociclib:er_positive coef=+1.574 (p<1e-300). Effect is unchanged by adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Pulled from the tamoxifen × er_positive adjusted model: er_positive main coef.
analyses.result_summary: er_positive coef = +0.548 mo (p=1.8e-285) after adjustment. Strong positive prognostic factor. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Pulled from the trastuzumab × her2_positive adjusted model: her2_positive main coef.
analyses.result_summary: her2_positive coef = -0.471 mo (p=3e-166). Strong negative prognostic factor independent of trastuzumab use. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pik3ca_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than pik3ca_mutation=0 patients (negative prognostic effect).
tp53_mutation=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than tp53_mutation=0 patients.

her2_amplification, msi_high, cdkn2a_loss, pten_loss, keap1_mutation, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, braf_v600e, ntrk_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration each associate with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: pik3ca_mutation delta = -0.559 mo (n=17350 vs 32650), p=6e-144. Strong negative prognostic effect.
tp53_mutation delta = +0.007, p=0.76. NO main effect — surprising, hypothesis REFUTED.

analyses.code: Per-feature Welch t-tests for the 12 genomic features.
analyses.result_summary: Only met_exon14_skipping delta=-0.144 (p=0.024) reached nominal significance, and would not survive multiple-testing correction (12 tests). All others p>=0.07. Hypothesis largely REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, and appetite_loss_grade are each associated with shorter pfs_months (negative effects).

analyses.result_summary: fatigue_grade coef=+0.0005 (p=0.96), pain_nrs -0.0015 (p=0.72), dyspnea_grade +0.010 (p=0.30), cough_grade -0.009 (p=0.38), appetite_loss_grade -0.002 (p=0.86). NO symptom severity score is associated with PFS. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: More heavily pre-treated patients (prior_chemotherapy=1, more prior_lines_of_therapy, longer years_since_diagnosis) have shorter pfs_months.
prior_immunotherapy=1 patients have shorter pfs_months (immune-experienced disease).

analyses.code: Per-feature t-tests / OLS for prior_chemotherapy, prior_radiation, prior_surgery, prior_targeted_therapy, prior_lines_of_therapy, years_since_diagnosis.
analyses.result_summary: Prior_chemotherapy delta=+0.002 (p=0.94), prior_radiation +0.045 (p=0.06), prior_surgery +0.066 (p=0.005, opposite direction — surgery slightly POSITIVE), prior_lines_of_therapy +0.009/line (p=0.34), years_since_diagnosis -0.003 (p=0.63). Hypothesis REFUTED — prior treatment burden does not shorten PFS in this cohort.
prior_immunotherapy delta = -0.001, p=0.98. NO effect. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one of the 24 pharmacogenomic SNPs (snp_rs1045642, ..., snp_rs4363657) shows a marginal association (p<0.05) with pfs_months as a main effect.

analyses.code: for snp in 24 snps: smf.ols('y ~ snp', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Of 24 SNPs tested individually, only snp_rs1801131 (MTHFR) reached nominal p<0.05 (coef=-0.054, p=0.026); next-best snp_rs2298771 p=0.082. With Bonferroni correction (alpha/24 = 0.0021), no SNP is significant. Effectively NULL — no pharmacogenomic main effect on PFS in this cohort. Hypothesis weakly supported (one nominal hit) but not robustly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment, pik3ca_mutation=1 modifies (REDUCES) the PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib relative to pik3ca_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
pik3ca_mutation modifies the PFS benefit of treatment_tamoxifen, _trastuzumab, _olaparib, _pembrolizumab, _sacituzumab_govitecan.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation coef = -1.668 mo (p<1e-300). PIK3CA mutation strongly ABROGATES palbociclib benefit. Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED — consistent with PI3K-pathway-driven resistance to CDK4/6 inhibition.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interactions for tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, pembrolizumab, sacituzumab × pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: All interactions non-significant: tamoxifen×pik3ca -0.013 (p=0.61), trastuzumab×pik3ca +0.012 (p=0.69), olaparib×pik3ca +0.035 (p=0.37), pembro×pik3ca +0.046 (p=0.16), sacituzumab×pik3ca -0.027 (p=0.50). PIK3CA selectively modifies palbociclib only. Hypothesis REFUTED for non-palbo treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way interaction treatment_palbociclib × er_positive × pik3ca_mutation: palbociclib produces a large PFS benefit ONLY in ER+/PIK3CA-wildtype patients, with the benefit abrogated when pik3ca_mutation=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ age+ecog+stage_iv + treatment_palbociclib*er_positive*pik3ca_mutation', data=df).fit(); also stratified mean PFS in 8 cells.
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coefficient = -2.370 mo (p<1e-300). Stratified means confirm a single 'sweet spot': ER+/PIK3CA-WT/palbo+ has mean PFS 6.68 mo (n=8034) vs ~4.30 mo in all 7 other cells (palbo-, ER-, or PIK3CA-mutated). Hypothesis STRONGLY SUPPORTED — palbociclib confers ~2.4-month benefit conditional on BOTH ER+ AND PIK3CA-WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Female (sex_female=1) patients have different pfs_months than male patients in this breast-cancer cohort.
Treatment receipt rates differ by sex (e.g., women receive more tamoxifen/palbociclib than men).

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind + adjusted OLS for sex_female on pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted delta=-0.013, p=0.56; adjusted coef=+0.006, p=0.65. NO sex effect on PFS (cohort is 44.7% female, 55.3% male — atypical for breast cancer). Hypothesis REFUTED.

Treatment receipt rates are essentially identical between sexes (e.g., palbociclib: 0.348 F vs 0.349 M; tamoxifen 0.301 vs 0.300; pembrolizumab 0.154 vs 0.150). NO sex-based treatment selection. Hypothesis REFUTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive age_years coefficient (+0.176 mo/year) is monotonic across deciles, not driven by an outlier age band.
After adjusting for ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, and albumin_g_dl, the positive age_years effect persists (i.e., it is not an artifact of healthier elderly).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS rises monotonically with age band: <40 → 0.55 mo (n=334), 40-55 → 2.13 mo (n=7558), 55-70 → 4.29 mo (n=26470), 70-90 → 6.64 mo (n=15318), 90+ → 9.92 mo (n=320). Hypothesis SUPPORTED — positive age effect is real and monotonic, with very large magnitude.

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef = +0.176 mo/yr (p<1e-300), essentially unchanged from unadjusted. NOT explained by ECOG, stage, brain mets, or albumin. Hypothesis SUPPORTED. Note: this contradicts typical clinical experience (younger breast cancer patients usually do better), suggesting either an unusual cohort or a synthetic-data artifact.

proposed_hypotheses.text: postmenopausal=1 patients have different pfs_months than premenopausal patients (independent of age).

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + postmenopausal', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: postmenopausal coef = -0.024 mo (p=0.066) after adjustment. Borderline negative effect. Univariate delta=-0.023 (p=0.31). Hypothesis essentially REFUTED — once age is in the model, postmenopausal status adds no signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ecog_ps and stage_iv interact: high ECOG is more harmful in stage_iv=1 patients than in stage_iv=0 patients (negative interaction, i.e., the stage-IV decrement is larger at higher ECOG).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction ecog_ps:stage_iv coef = +0.061 mo (p=0.003). Direction is OPPOSITE of hypothesized: the stage IV decrement is slightly SMALLER at higher ECOG (the two effects are sub-additive — when both are bad, the joint decrement is less than the sum). Hypothesis REFUTED in direction (though significant interaction exists).

proposed_hypotheses.text: has_brain_mets and stage_iv interact: brain metastases are more harmful in stage IV disease (negative interaction).
has_brain_mets prevalence is similar across stage_iv strata (suggesting brain mets are encoded independently of stage).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction stage_iv:has_brain_mets coef = -0.006 (p=0.93). NO interaction; brain mets and stage IV act additively. Hypothesis REFUTED.

Brain mets prevalence: 10.2% in stage_iv=0 vs 10.2% in stage_iv=1. Identical — suggesting brain_mets is encoded independently of stage in the data generating process (atypical clinically). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+ patients, the palbociclib PFS benefit is consistent (similar magnitude) across ecog_ps strata 0, 1, and 2.

analyses.code: Stratified mean PFS within ER+ subgroup, by ecog_ps and treatment_palbociclib.
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+ patients, palbociclib delta (vs no palbo): ECOG 0 = +1.59 mo (p=5e-195), ECOG 1 = +1.57 mo (p=3e-273), ECOG 2 = +1.64 mo (p=1e-94). Effect size is essentially identical across PS strata. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — palbociclib's ER+-conditional benefit does not require good performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+ patients, higher albumin_g_dl amplifies the palbociclib benefit (positive albumin × palbociclib interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('y ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl * treatment_palbociclib', data=df[df.er_positive==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+: interaction albumin × palbociclib coef = -0.068 mo per g/dL (p=0.034). Direction is OPPOSITE of hypothesis: palbociclib benefit is slightly LARGER at LOWER albumin. Hypothesis REFUTED in direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: brca1_mutation=1 and brca2_mutation=1 patients each have shorter pfs_months than wildtype (germline BRCA aggressive disease hypothesis).

analyses.code: Welch t-tests for brca1_mutation and brca2_mutation main effects on pfs_months.
analyses.result_summary: brca1_mutation delta = -0.024 (p=0.72, n=1277); brca2_mutation delta = -0.127 (p=0.081, n=1222). Both in negative direction but neither reaches significance individually. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within indication-matched subgroups, the targeted-therapy benefits expected from biology hold: palbociclib helps ER+, olaparib helps BRCA1/2, tamoxifen helps ER+, trastuzumab helps HER2+, pembrolizumab helps TNBC, sacituzumab helps TNBC — all positive deltas.

analyses.code: Within each indication subgroup, Welch t-test of pfs_months between treated and untreated.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup-restricted treatment deltas: (1) Palbociclib in ER+: +1.57 mo (p<1e-300) — STRONGLY SUPPORTED; (2) Olaparib in BRCA1/2-any: +0.35 mo (p=0.041, n_treated=239) — SUPPORTED at nominal p<0.05; (3) Tamoxifen in ER+: +0.023 (p=0.45) — REFUTED; (4) Trastuzumab in HER2+: -0.079 (p=0.15) — REFUTED; (5) Pembrolizumab in TNBC: +0.072 (p=0.22) — REFUTED; (6) Sacituzumab in TNBC: -0.085 (p=0.22) — REFUTED. Of six biology-motivated targeted therapies, only palbociclib in ER+ and (weakly) olaparib in BRCA1/2 carriers showed subgroup-confined benefit.
